_comment: Header-injection / response-splitting payloads. CRLF in a header value can cause naive frameworks to split the request or response and inject new headers. The Python requests library refuses to send raw CRLF in a header value (it raises InvalidHeader) - the runner catches this and records the payload as 'client-blocked' (treated as PASS since the attack didn't reach the server). PASS = 4xx, client-blocked, or sanitized header. FAIL = the injected header is reflected back into the response.

payloads.rationale: CRLF after a forwarded-for IP, injecting a Set-Cookie. requests library blocks this client-side; verifies the harness does not synthesize a corrupted request.
User-Agent + CRLF + privileged header. Stops cold at the client lib.
LF-only (no CR) injection - some lenient parsers accept this where strict CRLF gets caught.
NULL byte separator - exploits C-string truncation in upstream parsers.
Overly-long custom header NAME. Should be silently dropped or accepted, never 500. Not technically an injection but a header-budget probe.
